steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that putting a work online “increases the number of people who experience that work.” To support this claim, the correct finding must show that the online version reaches a larger audience than the physical work alone — more people experiencing it because of digitization.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Each year, more people access the online image of Self-Portrait than visit the Albertina Museum in person.': If online access exceeds in-person visits, the digital version has expanded the audience beyond what physical access alone would allow. This directly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Self-Portrait has been praised by some art critics.': Critical reception says nothing about whether digitization broadens who experiences the work. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Many people who have been to Vienna say that the Albertina Museum is worth visiting.': Addresses the museum's reputation as a travel destination, not the reach of its online images. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Albertina Museum has several works by Hendrick Goltzius.': Describes collection composition — irrelevant to whether putting a work online increases access. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
